title: Protocol I: Transfection of Salpingoeca rosetta with Lonza 4d nucleofector
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fnjl3","text":"The protocol describes the transformation of the choanoflagellate Salpingoeca rosetta using a Nucleofector 4d Device and SF kit from Lonza. Plasmids that use S. rosetta promoters and regulatory elements to drive the expression of luciferase and fluorescent protein reporters are being deposited at Addgene. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":66,"length":19},{"style":"italic","offset":158,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"985q8","text":"To maintain sterility, perform all steps in a biosafety cabinet unless otherwise noted.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"468s","text":" For protocols on starting and maintaining choanoflagellate cultures and preparing media, see King N, Young SL, Abedin M, Carr M, and  Leadbeater BSC (2009) Emerging Model Organisms, Vol. 1, CSHL Press and Levin TC and King N (2013) Current Biology 23:2176-80. \n ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":157,"length":44},{"style":"italic","offset":233,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"24mmg","text":" For seawater recipe, see Cembella AD (1995) Manual on Harmful Marine Microalgae, Chapter Three: Culture Methods. UNESCO (http://unesdoc.unesco.org/images/0013/001317/131711e.pdf)\n ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":45,"length":35}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Maintain a small (6 ml in 25 cm 2 vented flask) or medium (18 ml in 75 cm 2 vented flask) culture Salpingoeca rosetta feeding on Echinicola pacifica (SrEpac) grown in 5% Artificial Seawater Complete Media (ASWC). Count the concentration of cells using a haemocytometer. Use the starter culture to innoculate 200 ml of 5% ASW in a Falcon 5-layer flask to a final cell concentration of 5x10 3 cells/ml. Grow culture for 36-48 hours at 22°C and 60% humidity.

Split S. rosetta culture between four 50 ml conical tubes. Vigorously shake tubes in hand for 30 sec. Harvest cells by spinning at 2000 x g for 5 min at room temperature in a swinging bucket rotor. Pipette off supernatant with a 50 ml serological pipette, leaving a little bit of liquid on top of the pellet. Then use a fine tip transfer pipette to remove the rest of the liquid on the opposite side of the pellet. Resuspend each pellet in 25 ml of ASW and combine into two 50 ml conical tubes. Vigorously shake in hand for 30 sec. Spin cells at 2200 x g for 5 min at room temperature in a swinging bucket rotor. Pipette off supernatant with 50 ml serological pipette, leaving a little bit of liquid on top of the pellet. Then use a fine tip transfer pipette to remove the rest of the liquid on the opposite side of the pellet. Resuspend each pellet in 50 ml of ASW. Vigorously shake in hand for 30 sec. Spin cells at 2400 x g for 5 min at room temperature in a swinging bucket rotor. Pipette off supernatant with 50 ml serological pipette, leaving a little bit of liquid on top of the pellet. Then use a fine tip transfer pipette to remove the rest of the liquid on the opposite side of the pellet. Resupsend each cell pellet in 50 µl of ASW and combine them into one tube.

Add 2 µl of concentrated and washed S. rosetta cells to 196 µl of ASW. Fix cells with 2 µl of 37.5% (w/v) formaldehyde. Count cells on haemocytomer and calculate cell concentrations. Make sure to account for the 100x dilution factor. Dilute cells to a final concentration of 5x10 7 cells/ml with ASW. Aliquot 100 µl of cells to 1.5 ml centrifuge tubes. Each aliquot contains 5x10 6 cells.

Centrifuge 100 µl aliquots of 5x10 6 cells at 2750 x g and room temperature in a table-top centrifuge with a fixed-angle rotor for 5 min. If your centrifuge has the option, turn off the brake or set to a soft setting. This prevents cells from experiencing high centrifugal forces. During the centrifugation, add 10 µl of 100 µM papain prepared in Step 3 to 1 ml of pretreatment buffer (Protocol E). Mix well by pipetting up and down. After centrifugation, remove supernatant with a fine pipette tip. Resuspend the cells pellets in 100 µl of in pretreatment buffer with papain. Incubate cells at room temperature for 30 min. Quench reaction by adding 1 µl of 50 mg/ml BSA (Protocol D). Mix well by pipetting up and down. Centrifuge cells at 1250 x g and room temperature in a table-top centrifuge with a fixed-angle rotor for 5 min. Again, use the soft brake setting or no brake at all. Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 25 µl of SF Buffer (Lonza)

Add 16 µl of SF Buffer to DNA mixture prepared in step 2. Total volume should be 21 µl Add 2 µl of pretreated cells the DNA mixture diluted in SF Buffer. Mix well by pipetting up and down. Pipette 23 µl of the complete nucleofection mixture to one well of a 96-well nucleofection plate. Prepare recovery buffer (Protocol F) to add immediately after nucleofection. For nucleofecting multiple samples, pour buffer into a liquid reservoir Transfect cells by applying pulse CM-156 with a Lonza nucleofector.

Dilute papain to a final concentration of 100 µM in dilution buffer [50 mM HEPES-KOH pH 7.5, 200 mM NaCl, 20% (v/v) glycerol, and 10 mM cysteine] from a stock solution of 1 mM papain (Sigma Aldrich). • Prepare a solution of of 50 mM HEPES-KOH pH 7.5, 200 mM NaCl, 20% (v/v) glycerol and 10 mM cysteine. • Sterile filtered through a 0.22 µm filter. • Prepare a ten fold dilution of dilute papain to a final concentration of 100 µM from a stock solution of 1 mM papain (Sigma Aldrich P3125-100mg). Pipette 20 µl of 1 mM papain stock a180 µl of 50 mM HEPES-KOH pH 7.5, 200 mM NaCl, and 20% (v/v) glycerol. • NOTE: Reducing reagents such as cysteine should be prepared fresh or extra care should be taken to maintain their reductive potential

For one nucleofection reaction, prepare one tube with 2 µl of 20 µg/µl of pUC19 carrier plasmid (Protocol A),1 µl of 250 mM adenosine triphosphate-NaOH, pH 7.5 (Protocol B), 1 µl of 100 mg/ml heparin (Protocol C), 1 µl of 2.5-10 µg/µl reporter plasmid (Protocol G). To save time, prepare a 400 µl master mix of addtives for nucleofection by mixing 200 µl of 20 µg/µl of pUC19 carrier plasmid, 100 µl of 250 mM adenosine triphosphate-NaOH, pH 7.5, 100 µl of 100 mg/ml Heparin. Add 4 µl of this master mix to 1 µl of reporter plasmid. This step can be performed outside of the hood. Take care to use sterile filtered solutions of ATP and Heparin. The amount of reporter plasmid should be adjusted by performing a dilution series to determine the best concentration for your particular assay. For nanoluc reporter assays, we use 2.5 µg of reporter plasmid. For fluorescence microscopy, we use 10 µg of total reporter plasmid

Immediately after nucleofection, add 100 µl of ice-cold recovery buffer (Protocol F) Incubate the cells in recovery buffer for 5 min at room temperature. Tap gently on the sides of the plate to mix the nucleofected cells with buffer. Gently pipette cells up and down and then transfer cells to 1 ml of 5% ASWC media. One hour after transferring cells to media, add 20 µl of a frozen 10 mg pellet of E. pacifica resuspended in 1 ml of ASW. Incubate for 48 h at 22°C and 60% humidity. After this time, cells will be ready for fluorescence microscopy or reporter assays.
creator: David Booth
